---
name: Data anomaly
about: A number on the site looks scientifically wrong, uses the wrong baseline, or disagrees with a more authoritative source
title: "[data] "
labels: data-anomaly
---

## Which metric

<!-- e.g. "Atmospheric CO₂", "Arctic sea-ice extent", "PM2.5 (global urban average)" -->

## What the site shows

<!-- Paste the value and the timestamp from the data-status panel. A screenshot is fine. -->

```
value:
unit:
fetchedAt:
source shown on site:
```

## What you believe it should show

<!-- The value you expected, or the range you expected. -->

## Authoritative reference

<!--
Link to the agency, paper, or dataset you think is more authoritative, and quote the figure.
If you think the *baseline* is wrong (e.g. GISTEMP 1951–1980 vs IPCC 1850–1900), say so here.
-->

- Reference URL:
- Publication / agency:
- DOI (if any):
- Quoted figure:
- Baseline / reference period (if relevant):

## Your assessment

<!--
Pick one or more:
- [ ] The site's numerical value is wrong.
- [ ] The site's numerical value is right but the baseline label is wrong.
- [ ] The site's numerical value is right but the unit is wrong.
- [ ] The site's numerical value is right but should be blended with another source.
- [ ] The metric should be deprecated in favour of a successor.
- [ ] Other: __________
-->

## Affiliation / COI

<!--
Optional but helpful for methodological reviews.
If you work for the originating agency, a competing dataset, or a climate-adjacent organisation, please say so.
-->

## Additional context

<!-- Anything else we should know. -->
